title: Agency Site Manager Takeover
concept: A dense, manager-first Playground redesign for agency developers where the Site Manager is the primary product surface, saved sites and start sources live in a left client queue, and the WordPress site is reduced to a narrow verification preview.
focus: Site Manager as the main workspace
client-site queue instead of modal-first navigation
monochrome operational UI with colored status chips
flows: Create a Playground from vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, or .zip import through the left start-source queue and contextual drawer.
Save a temporary Playground in browser storage or to a local directory with visible copy progress.
Browse unsaved and saved Playgrounds, then save, rename, or delete entries from the management table.
Use Site Manager tabs for overview, settings, files, Blueprint, database, and logs while keeping a narrow live WordPress preview visible.
Browse and filter the Blueprint gallery with featured blueprint cards, then return to the manager workspace.
Navigate the active WordPress path, refresh the preview, open Homepage or WP Admin, export to GitHub, and download a .zip package.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
